poem.content:
In the urban sprawl, where city lights gleam,
A throw of shadows dances, an eerie dream.
The roar of the crowd, a cacophony so bright,
A roast of memories, where love and heartache take flight.

I extend my thumb, a gesture of my past,
Echoes of moments that will forever last.
In the fiction of my mind, I weave a tapestry so fine,
A world where benefit and harm entwine.

But there's a wrong turn, a path I've yet to find,
A magnet that pulls, a heart and mind entwined.
The avocado of life, a fruit so green and fair,
Forgotten in the rush, a treasure beyond compare.

Below the surface, a world of secrets lies,
Fuel for the fire, that burns with anxious sighs.
I try to solve the riddle, of this urban grind,
To find a way to thrive, where love and heartache entwine.

In the picture of my mind, a vision starts to form,
A world where love and joy, forever will transform.
But for now, I'll hold on tight, to the can of hope,
A fragile thread, that keeps me from the endless rope.

In the darkness of the night, I search for a light,
A guiding star, that will lead me through the fight.
And when the morning comes, and the city starts to roar,
I'll rise above, and find my way to something more.
